task_completion.description: Navigate to the OpenCAD login page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Web browser open to http://localhost showing the OpenCAD login screen.

task_completion.description: Log in to OpenCAD using the provided admin credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenCAD dashboard or authenticated admin area is visible, indicating successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Impound Reasons management section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenCAD interface displays a list, table, or management page specifically for 'Impound Reasons'.

task_completion.description: Initiate the deletion of the 'Obstructing Machinery' impound reason.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A deletion confirmation prompt, modal, or a cursor clicking a delete button specifically targeting the 'Obstructing Machinery' entry.

task_completion.description: Confirm the 'Obstructing Machinery' impound reason has been successfully deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification indicating the reason was deleted, or the impound reasons list is visible and 'Obstructing Machinery' is no longer present in the list.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with OpenCAD.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) being used to navigate the site, rather than terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQL commands, database client tools, or scripts being used to delete the record directly from the backend.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the live OpenCAD application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The URL bar shows http://localhost and the interface matches a functional web application, not a text editor modifying source code or HTML files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must log in to OpenCAD at http://localhost using email 'admin@opencad.local' and password 'Admin123!'. The specific impound reason that must be deleted from the system is 'Obstructing Machinery'.
